Title: Traveling Salesman — City / Neighborhood / Address Semantic Geometry
Statement: Given finite city-scale weighted graphs, represent graph normalization, supplied tours, typed frontier obligations, local-to-global lower bounds, finite optimality certificates, and residual search as executable structural data.
Scope: Primary scope is the five-stop symmetric complete Gridville fixture with a fixed depot and explicit canonical undirected edge rows. A three-stop count-preserving broken graph and two invalid tour witnesses are negative certificates. The scope validates supplied cycles and proves the reference route optimal for this finite instance; it does not yet reconstruct the route from inference or provide a general solver. Loops 587-596 are recorded before execution with explicit before-state and closure criteria.
TrustBoundary: InputGraphKernel: city/neighborhood/address membership, required-stop selection, and TravelEdges weights are trusted input data. Pair identity, cycle coverage, local two-cheapest witnesses, double-counting, and bound equality are represented internally. Live Postgres commissioning remains an open substrate obligation.

Loops.Result: City -> Neighborhood -> Address hierarchy closed for the fixture.
Five-stop complete symmetric weighted graph normalized as ten canonical edges.
Candidate tours represented as ordered stops plus edge-bound legs.
Valid reference route accepted; duplicate-stop route rejected; optimality remained open.
Baseline recorded twelve symmetry-reduced route-discovery classes.
Frontier obligations typed separately from imported dependencies; imported count remains zero.
Global one-in/one-out transition coverage rejects a locally legal but globally incomplete cycle.
Canonical pair multiplicity rejects a count-preserving duplicate-edge graph.
Degree-two local bounds certify a global tour lower bound of 14.
Reference candidate cost 14 equals the certified lower bound, proving finite-instance optimality.

Loops.Result: Represented 5 applications, 5 antecedents, 5 selected edge states, and 10 supports; no edge was labeled FORCED.

Loops.BeforeState: Local degree bounds identify edges independently at stops, but their deduplicated union is not a first-class object.

Loops.Result: Derived 5 unique edges ['edge-a-b', 'edge-a-e', 'edge-b-c', 'edge-c-d', 'edge-d-e'] at total cost 14; no CandidateTour/TourStop/TourLeg row was an antecedent.

Loops.BeforeState: The inferred Gridville edge union has no represented degree, component, spanning-tree, or subtour certificate.

Loops.AfterState: All five stops have selected degree two; component count=1, spanning-tree edges=4, proper subtours=0.
Loops.Result: All five stops have selected degree two; component count=1, spanning-tree edges=4, proper subtours=0.

Loops.BeforeState: Gridville has a certified cycle edge set, but the only ordered route rows are supplied candidates.

Loops.Result: Reconstructed stop-a -> stop-b -> stop-c -> stop-d -> stop-e -> stop-a at cost 14; CandidateUsedAsAntecedent=false and the supplied route is comparison-only.

Loops.BeforeState: Route-discovery counts are recorded as baseline data and are not tied to a structural reconstruction certificate.

Loops.AfterState: Derived 12 initial route classes, one surviving reconstructed class, zero branch decisions, zero backtracks, and zero residual ambiguity.
Loops.Result: Derived 12 initial route classes, one surviving reconstructed class, zero branch decisions, zero backtracks, and zero residual ambiguity.

Loops.BeforeState: The only positive fixture has a tight degree-two lower bound, so failure behavior is not represented.
Loops.ClosureCriterion: Represent six stops in two cheap triangles with expensive crossings; certify lower bound 6, candidate cost 24, two selected components, and no optimality or route-reconstruction certificate.
Loops.AfterState: Twin triangles: certified degree-two lower bound 6, feasible candidate cost 24, selected component count 2, proper subtours 2; no optimality or reconstruction certificate emitted.
Loops.Result: Twin triangles: certified degree-two lower bound 6, feasible candidate cost 24, selected component count 2, proper subtours 2; no optimality or reconstruction certificate emitted.

Loops.BeforeState: Degree-two forcing is declared but has no executable state, round, or decision certificates.
Loops.ClosureCriterion: On a sparse five-stop fixture, stops with exactly two available incident edges force the five cycle edges with zero branching.
Loops.AfterState: Sparse fixture forced five ring edges from degree-two stops with zero branch decisions; B-D remains for saturation propagation.
Loops.Result: Sparse fixture forced five ring edges from degree-two stops with zero branch decisions; B-D remains for saturation propagation.

Loops.BeforeState: Forced edges do not yet emit deterministic forbidden-edge consequences or premature-subtour exclusions.
Loops.ClosureCriterion: Forbid the sparse B-D chord by degree saturation and forbid a twin-triangle closing edge because it would close a proper subtour, with replayable reason codes.
Loops.AfterState: Forbade sparse B-D by degree saturation and twin WA-WC by proper-subtour prevention; both decisions are deterministic and branch-free.
Loops.Result: Forbade sparse B-D by degree saturation and twin WA-WC by proper-subtour prevention; both decisions are deterministic and branch-free.

Loops.BeforeState: Neighborhood rows are geographic labels only; no executable entry/exit path states or contraction certificate exists.
Loops.ClosureCriterion: For each three-stop twin-triangle neighborhood, derive three undirected entry/exit Hamiltonian-path states, reduce six internal orders to three states, and reduce 36 paired internal orders to nine paired boundary-state combinations.

CurrentCertificates.Conclusion: tsp-gridville-5 has five stops and exactly one admissible canonical edge for every unordered pair.
tsp-gridville-broken-3 has the expected edge-row count but is incomplete because AB is duplicated and BC is absent.
A-B-C-D-E-A is a valid supplied Hamiltonian cycle of cost 14.
A-B-C-B-E-A is rejected because B is repeated and D is omitted.
A candidate with a duplicated A-B transition and missing E-A transition is rejected despite five unique visits and five locally legal legs.
Local bounds 5+5+6+6+6 double-count tour edges, yielding a certified global lower bound of 14.
The valid reference route cost 14 equals the certified lower bound 14, so the route is optimal for tsp-gridville-5.

RemainingFrontier: live Postgres build and Python/Postgres conformance with artifact hashes
route reconstruction from inferred edge rows rather than a supplied candidate
degree-two forced-edge closure after admissibility pruning
neighborhood boundary-state inference and component contraction
subtour exclusion certificates for unordered inferred edge sets
non-tight lower-bound fixtures requiring stronger inference or explicit residual branching

Vocabulary.ImportedDependency: An external provider conclusion consumed without internal derivation in this domain.
Vocabulary.FrontierObligation: Any still-open semantic edge, typed as inference, certificate, substrate, generalization, or residual search.
Vocabulary.ResidualSearch: Explicit ambiguity remaining after all currently represented deterministic obligations reach closure.
Vocabulary.KernelAssumption: Trusted primitive input semantics recorded at the trust boundary, not silently promoted into a derivation.
